title: cgroups: check version using c/common
body:
This commit reuses the cgroups version check logic from c/common and helps to get rid of the duplicated check in util/util_linux.go.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind cleanup

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

uses cgroup version check from c/common and removes dup code.

#### How to verify it

Nothing breaks compared to before.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

number or None on one line.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

None

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@nalind was cgroups version check duplicated in buildah to avoid pulling in ebpf ? If that's the case I wonder if this PR is worth continuing

`FreeBSD 386` cross compile fails with:
```
GOOS=freebsd GOARCH=386 GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor -ldflags '-X main.GitCommit=f2a11e62450870ec09fbd997b58c783eb1d5bdb3-dirty -X main.buildInfo=1707477409 -X main.cniVersion=v1.1.2 ' -o bin/buildah.freebsd.386 -tags "containers_image_openpgp" ./cmd/buildah
# github.com/godbus/dbus/v5
vendor/github.com/godbus/dbus/v5/transport_unix.go:52:10: cannot use t (variable of type *unixTransport) as transport value in return statement: *unixTransport does not implement transport (missing method SendNullByte)
vendor/github.com/godbus/dbus/v5/transport_unix.go:58:10: cannot use t (variable of type *unixTransport) as transport value in return statement: *unixTransport does not implement transport (missing method SendNullByte)
```

Do we need to support all os-arch combos in the `cross` target?

EDIT: s/distro-arch/os-arch/
@nalind @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL. How big of a concern is disabling freebsd cross compile?
@lsm5 as we have @dfr and others contributing for BSD, I'd say it's at least a solid concern.  I'd rather not disable it unless avoidable.  @dfr do you have a differing opinion?

> @lsm5 as we have @dfr and others contributing for BSD, I'd say it's at least a solid concern. I'd rather not disable it unless avoidable. @dfr do you have a differing opinion?

ack, then we can't afford the Makefile change. I'll look further into this

> @lsm5 as we have @dfr and others contributing for BSD, I'd say it's at least a solid concern. I'd rather not disable it unless avoidable. @dfr do you have a differing opinion?

I certainly want Buildah to keep working on FreeBSD - I use it for my own work and support it and Podman for other FreeBSD users. The build error is in github.com/godbus/dbus - can we avoid this for non-linux in some way? It seems likely this comes from the cgroups_unsupported.go importing github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22/dbus for cgroups.UserConnection.

On the other hand, godbus/dbus/v5 should at least build on FreeBSD. I fixed some nocgo in it problems back in 2022 but the version in common's vendor directory doesn't seem to have that fix.

Edit: It looks like Podman has a version of godbus/dbus/v5 which does have my fix and which builds on FreeBSD both with and without cgo.
